title: Been using an AI stock research tool for a month. Honest review from someone who went in skeptical
author: Scary-Alternative-81
selftext:
I'll be upfront, I used Multibagg fully expecting to write a post about how it's just another chatbot with a stock market skin.

That's not where I ended up.

I'm not a serious trader. Salaried, check my portfolio more than I should, read annual reports. I've tried Screener and Trendlyne. They're fine. I use them more as data dumps and do the thinking myself.

What I actually use Multibagg for is cross-checking my own thesis. I'll have a reason to buy something, ask the tool to push back on it, and see what comes up. I was  about to buy a company where the promoter pledging data was worse than I'd noticed. Just... sitting there in the summary, obvious, while I was busy getting excited about the revenue number.

The earnings call transcript thing genuinely changed how I work. I used to spend an hour on a transcript and retain two things. Now I get a summary that separates what management said from what they were probably trying to spin. I don't know how much I trust that framing 100% of the time but it's a useful starting point.

I still verify anything before I actually put money on it. But the tool has made the part before that, the reading, the scanning, the "wait what does this number mean" part a lot faster.

Didn't expect to keep using it.

comments.author: The_AI_Trader
comments.body: AI is the way to go. Nice job. Keep putting them into your processes.

comments.author: PurpleReaction8004
comments.body: AI stock research tools like Multibagg or Prospero AI, Moomoo and other stock screener apps are the future of investing which is making it easier for someone like you who not as serious about trading. Good luck
